title: JSON field aggregation to sorted key=value lines
prompt: You are given a JSON object describing an order.
total_qty     = the sum of all item quantities
distinct_skus = the number of distinct sku values
Emit exactly three lines of the form key=value:
sorted in ascending alphabetical order by key name (as shown above).
Output ONLY the three lines between <answer> and </answer> tags.
difficulty_rationale: Parse-and-aggregate over a tiny fixed JSON. One sum, one set-count, one lookup, plus a specified sort order. Marginally above T1a because of the aggregation, still fully mechanical and unambiguous.
